notes: v1.14.33 — landing globe flipped to NIGHT (brief §4, hybrid lane). The Earth shader (gprog) drops the day/night sun mix entirely: terrain is the Blue Marble albedo multiplied down hard (~0.13-0.22) and cool-shifted so landmasses read faintly in shadow with near-black oceans, clouds go faint and unlit, and the atmosphere rim is a constant cool-blue fresnel — no daylit hemisphere, no warm terminator, sun killed. The NASA Black Marble baked city lights are suppressed; instead the SPECTRUM emissive city-light dots (cyan-violet-magenta, real nightLevel-mask placement, twinkle + limb occlusion) are re-enabled (SHOW_SPECTRUM_LAND=true) as the ONLY glow. Violet coastline-outline dots stay OFF (new SHOW_SPECTRUM_BORDERS=false) so they do not fight the texture's real coastlines. Network footprint trimmed to the real CoreWeave NA+Europe set (Austin home + Plano/Chicago/Weehawken/Chester/Douglasville/Hillsboro/Las Vegas/Reno/London-Crawley); APAC/LATAM nodes removed. ARCS rewritten: Austin radiates to every node + three trans-Atlantic spans to London. Hold-to-enter / warp-to-Austin (index 0) unchanged. DEVICE-GATE (John, iPhone): confirm night not day, tune SPECTRUM emission so dense clusters do not pile to white on DPR3 OLED, size gl_PointSize on device (~2x desktop), verify rotation/packets subtle + frame rate holds. node --check 3/3 + sw.js PASS, stamps lockstep v1.14.33.
